us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Judge blocks DOJ bid for Fulton County 2020 election worker data
bullets: A federal judge rejected the Justice Department’s subpoena for names and contact information of Fulton County election workers.
The subpoena covered county employees and volunteer poll workers from Georgia’s 2020 election.
The judge called the request unreasonable and overly broad.

articles.title: Maine Senate race thrown into turmoil by allegation against Graham Platner
bullets: Democratic nominee Graham Platner faces calls to leave the race after a sexual assault allegation.
The race against Republican Sen. Susan Collins could affect control of the U.S. Senate.
AP reported that Maine Democrats could replace Platner on the ballot if he exits by July 13.

articles.title: Manhattan high-rise stabilized after buckled columns prompted evacuations
bullets: A midtown Manhattan tower under construction was stabilized late Tuesday.
Nearby buildings, including a school and hotels, were evacuated after columns buckled.
The former Pfizer headquarters is being converted into luxury apartments.

articles.title: Defense challenges DNA evidence in Charlie Kirk assassination case
bullets: Lawyers for Tyler Robinson questioned FBI DNA testing tied to the suspected murder weapon.
Prosecutors say Robinson is linked to a rifle found at Utah Valley University.
A weeklong hearing continued as prosecutors seek to move the aggravated murder case forward.

articles.title: ICE agent fatally shoots man during Houston enforcement operation
bullets: ICE said an officer shot and killed a man during a targeted enforcement operation in Houston.
The agency said the man attempted to evade arrest in a vehicle.
AP reported it was at least the eighth death from an encounter with federal immigration officials in the current enforcement campaign.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Major worldwide developments from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: US-Iran ceasefire collapses after Gulf attacks and new US strikes
bullets: Trump said the interim agreement with Iran was over after Iranian attacks on U.S. military sites in the Gulf.
The U.S. launched new strikes on Iran after tanker attacks in the Strait of Hormuz.
Oil prices rose sharply as the confrontation threatened shipping and energy markets.

articles.title: NATO showcases arms deals as Trump renews Greenland demand
bullets: NATO used its Ankara summit to announce new defense projects and aircraft purchases.
Trump again said the United States should control Greenland rather than Denmark.
The summit also featured discussion of Turkey sanctions and Ukraine’s NATO ambitions.

articles.title: Marine Le Pen says she will run for French presidency despite monitoring order
bullets: Le Pen confirmed a new presidential run after a French court ruling in her embezzlement case.
Reuters reported the ruling allowed her to campaign while wearing an electronic tag.
She said she would appeal to France’s highest court.

articles.title: Nigel Farage to resign as MP and seek reelection amid donation scrutiny
bullets: Farage said he would quit his Parliament seat and stand again in a by-election.
A standards watchdog is investigating a £5 million donation linked to Christopher Harborne.
Farage denied wrongdoing and said the move was meant to clear his name.

articles.title: Ukraine says drones hit Russian refineries, tankers and pipeline sites
bullets: Ukrainian and Russian officials reported a major overnight wave of drone strikes.
Reuters said targets included three Russian oil refineries and tankers on the Sea of Azov.
The strikes extended from border areas toward the Urals region.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top India-focused stories from the last 1–2 days

articles.title: Heavy rain disrupts Delhi-NCR; Gurugram offices advised to use work from home
bullets: Heavy rain caused waterlogging and traffic congestion in Gurugram and nearby areas.
Police advised corporate offices to implement work-from-home arrangements.
Delhi, Noida and Ghaziabad were under rain alerts during the day.

articles.title: July downpours narrow India’s rainfall deficit to 12%
bullets: PTI reported that India’s monsoon rainfall deficit narrowed to 12%.
Above-normal rain in early July helped reduce the shortfall.
Officials reviewed preparedness for possible El Niño effects on the kharif season.

articles.title: Rupee weakens as oil rally revives pressure from Middle East tensions
bullets: The rupee traded slightly weaker Wednesday as oil prices rose.
Reuters reported traders pointed to modest portfolio inflows that cushioned the fall.
Higher crude prices remain a key risk for India’s import bill and currency outlook.

articles.title: Mumbai airport avoids runway incident involving two Air India aircraft
bullets: The Indian Express reported a near-miss involving an Air India flight and an Air India Express aircraft.
Air Traffic Control intervened before any collision occurred.
No injuries or damage were reported, and authorities had not yet explained the runway conflict.

articles.title: Baruipur rape-murder accused shot dead during police encounter
bullets: Police said the accused in the rape and murder of an 11-year-old girl was shot dead during crime-scene reconstruction.
Authorities said he snatched a firearm and fired at police before retaliatory firing.
The case had sparked intense local tension in West Bengal.

articles.title: US stock futures retreat as oil jumps after Iran escalation
bullets: AP reported S&P 500 futures fell 0.7%, Dow futures slid 1% and Nasdaq futures lost 1.1% before the open.
Brent crude jumped to $78.10 a barrel after U.S.-Iran tensions escalated.
The move followed Trump’s statement that the interim Iran agreement was over.

articles.title: TeraWulf rallies on $19 billion Anthropic data-center lease
bullets: TeraWulf signed a 20-year lease with Anthropic for AI data-center infrastructure.
Reuters said the deal is expected to generate about $19 billion in contracted revenue.
The company’s shares rose more than 16% in premarket trading after the announcement.

articles.title: Nvidia slips after report DeepSeek is building its own AI chip
bullets: Reuters reported DeepSeek is developing an AI inference chip.
Investing.com said Nvidia shares slipped in premarket trading after the report.
The project could reduce DeepSeek’s reliance on Nvidia and Huawei hardware.

articles.title: Micron, Western Digital and Sandisk drop as chip concerns pressure Nasdaq futures
bullets: Reuters reported Nasdaq futures fell as investors reassessed the AI chip rally.
Micron, Western Digital and Sandisk were among memory-linked stocks trading lower premarket.
Samsung’s record profit failed to calm concerns about chip valuations.

articles.title: Microsoft shares in focus after 4,800 job cuts across Xbox and commercial units
bullets: Microsoft announced cuts equal to about 2.1% of its global workforce.
AP reported the reductions included many workers in Xbox and gaming-related divisions.
The company said Xbox expects additional job cuts during the fiscal year.

articles.title: MasTec to buy Superior Group in $1.65 billion data-center infrastructure deal
bullets: MasTec agreed to acquire electrical contractor Superior Group for $1.65 billion.
Reuters said the deal expands MasTec’s data-center and mission-critical infrastructure capabilities.
MasTec shares rose in extended trading after the announcement.

articles.title: Anthropic signs long-term AI infrastructure lease with TeraWulf
bullets: The 20-year lease covers a purpose-built AI campus in Hawesville, Kentucky.
The site is expected to support about 401 megawatts of critical IT load.
TeraWulf is also selling its stake in the Abernathy joint venture to fund AI infrastructure projects.

articles.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs as it restructures Xbox and commercial operations
bullets: Microsoft is eliminating about 4,800 roles globally.
The cuts include a large number of jobs tied to Xbox and gaming.
The company framed the move as part of a broader reset while technology and work patterns shift.

articles.title: Trump says he will lift Turkey sanctions and decide on F-35 sale
bullets: Trump said he would lift sanctions on Turkey during a NATO summit meeting with President Tayyip Erdogan.
Reuters reported Trump would also decide on a potential F-35 sale to Ankara.
The issue could affect defense contractors and U.S.-Turkey military ties.

articles.title: NATO arms announcements spotlight Saab, Airbus and surveillance aircraft deals
bullets: NATO allies announced several defense procurement projects at the Ankara summit.
Sweden said Saab would supply up to 10 GlobalEye surveillance aircraft for a 10-nation consortium.
AP also reported a multinational effort involving Airbus refueling and transport planes.

articles.title: DeepSeek developing its own AI inference chip, Reuters reports
bullets: Reuters said China’s DeepSeek is designing a chip for AI inference workloads.
The effort could reduce reliance on Nvidia and Huawei chips.
Nvidia shares slipped after the report became public.

articles.title: Samsung’s record AI-memory profit fails to prevent chip stock selloff
bullets: Samsung reported a major profit surge driven by AI memory demand.
Investors still sold chip stocks as concerns grew about the sustainability of the AI rally.
South Korean memory names Samsung and SK Hynix fell in early Wednesday trading.

articles.title: UN holds first Global Dialogue on AI Governance in Geneva
bullets: The United Nations convened its first Global Dialogue on AI Governance on July 6–7 in Geneva.
The forum brought governments and stakeholders together to discuss international AI cooperation.
UN officials linked the meeting to broader work on AI safety, access and accountability.

articles.title: Microsoft says 4,800 layoffs are not direct AI replacements as work shifts
bullets: Microsoft eliminated about 4,800 roles across the company.
Windows Central reported the company said the roles were not being directly replaced by AI.
The memo still described AI as changing how work gets done.

articles.title: SpaceX added to Nasdaq-100 index after public-market debut
bullets: Nasdaq announced Space Exploration Technologies would join the Nasdaq-100 beginning July 7.
AP market coverage said SpaceX fell 6.8% in its first day of trading after inclusion.
The listing added a major aerospace-and-AI name to the tech-heavy benchmark.
